target.name: Black Forest Labs
target.founder: Robin Rombach, Andreas Blattmann, Patrick Esser

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Black Forest Labs, an AI & Software company founded by Robin Rombach, Andreas Blattmann, and Patrick Esser, is a Series B-stage private firm currently valued above $1B with $300M raised to date. Valuation benchmarking draws on a peer set of 5 same-stage, same-sector private comparables (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom) ranging from $500M to $32.0B in valuation, plus 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method, using $300M total raised as a proxy base and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies $570M. The Peer-Median method across the 5-company private comparable set yields a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The asymmetry in the peer range—driven by Safe Superintelligence at the high end—reflects substantial heterogeneity within frontier-stage AI valuations.

Triangulation across these two methods produces a valuation range of $484M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.8B. The stated valuation above $1B falls within this range. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the stage-progression multiplier given AI sector momentum, the relevance of the $32B outlier (Safe Superintelligence), and revenue-generation timelines for generative AI models, which remain nascent across the peer set.
